message:
style(fcp-auth-schema): rustfmt pass on claims.rs (signature wraps, assert arg splits)

Mechanical rustfmt output, no semantic changes. Four locations in
crates/fcp-auth-schema/src/claims.rs:

- `fn expect_array(label: i64, v: ciborium::Value) -> Result<...>`
  — signature collapsed onto a single line; the one-arg-per-line
  wrap was exceeding rustfmt's `fn_call_width` threshold only
  because of the argument list, and the collapsed form fits within
  the crate's 100-column budget.
- `utc(year, month, day)` helper in `mod tests`: the
  `Utc.with_ymd_and_hms(..).single().unwrap()` chain is split across
  three lines instead of two — matches the style rustfmt applies to
  other `.single().unwrap()` call chains in the file.
- `empty_emits_only_schema_version` assert: the
  `"empty() must emit only schema_version, got {entries:?}"` message
  gets its own line, and the three `assert_eq!` args are each on
  their own line. Same assertion, same message.
- `exp_timestamp_out_of_range_carries_value_for_audit` assert: the
  `assert_eq!(value, i64::MAX, "value must be the actual
  out-of-range timestamp, not 0")` has its first two args on
  separate lines for clarity.
- `check_schema_version_rejects_unknown_version`: the method-chain
  `c.check_schema_version(&[CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION]).unwrap_err()` is
  spread onto three lines instead of two.

No public surface changes; no logic changes. `cargo fmt --check`
on this file now passes on the default toolchain.
